hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 5, K. You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — 3, Queen, Queen. You bet. Your opponent just called.
Turn — Queen. You bet again. Your opponent raised you.

wrongReasons: A 4 has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with A 4.
Q 3 makes a four of a kind here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
2 7 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only three of a kind wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 3 2

Your opponent flopped a full house and slow-played the flop, calling to disguise their strength. Once the pot was big enough they raised the turn and shoved the river — a classic trap with a monster hand.
